# Perspectives showcase: named-layout morphing + shared-heap window pop-out demo

## Context

Operator seed (2026-07-04, live): the second of the ‚â•2 stunning public demos for Lane 3 (D#14561). This is **Demo B ‚Äî the only-Neo one**: named perspectives morphing with animation, and a pane detaching to its own OS window while its running state survives, because both windows live on ONE SharedWorker App-Worker heap. No competing docking library can show this; it is the object-permanence story made visible.

## The Problem

Demo A (choreography, #14589) shows polish on shipped ops; it does not show the differentiator. The multi-window + perspectives capability is exactly what the industry's single-process docking libraries (Qt-ADS, Dockview, GoldenLayout) structurally cannot do ‚Äî and it is currently invisible. This demo is also the FM-cockpit's pop-out story (#14560 consumes the same seam) told as a public asset.

## The Architectural Reality

- **Spec-gated by design:** perspective semantics (capture scope, cross-topology restore, fail-closed behavior) and cross-window drag are ¬ß2/¬ß3 of the #14423 design spec ‚Äî unsettled until it lands. This ticket is `blocked_by` #14423 and consumes its contracts; filing now records the operator-seeded shape so the demo starts the day the spec merges.
- Substrate: Chromium popups on one SharedWorker heap (ADR 0020 vessel); the persistence wrapper (`neo.harness.dockLayout.v1`) models `layoutId`/`title`/`revision`; seam-v0 (#14423 comment) already promises reparent-never-recreate.
- Placement: structural-pre-flight at claim (likely composes WITH Demo A's surface rather than a third app ‚Äî the sibling sweep decides).

## The Fix

One PR (post-spec):

1. **Perspective morphing**: 3‚Äì4 named layouts (e.g. "Focus", "Review", "Fleet", "Presentation") with animated transitions between them ‚Äî capture/restore per spec ¬ß2, incl. one deliberate cross-topology restore (fewer windows than captured) rendering the spec's fail-closed behavior honestly.
2. **The pop-out moment**: a live pane (ticking state ‚Äî clock/stream/counter) detaches to an OS window and reattaches, state continuous, never remounted. The camera-friendly beat of the whole lane.
3. **Tour mode** (same contract as Demo A): deterministic NL-driven run for recording.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Perspectives morph with animated transitions; capture/restore semantics match spec ¬ß2 exactly (spec cited per behavior).
- [ ] Cross-topology restore is demonstrated and renders the fail-closed path visibly (no silent divergence).
- [ ] The pop-out pane provably never remounts (state continuity assertable ‚Äî consumed by the e2e leaf).
- [ ] Fully NL-drivable; tour deterministic across two runs.
- [ ] Public-safe; fresh-checkout runnable.
- [ ] Cross-family review before merge.

## Out of Scope

- Settling perspective/cross-window semantics (that IS #14423).
- Grouped drag + overflow (its own future leaf per the spec).
- e2e specs (sibling leaf extends to this demo post-merge).

## Related

Parent: #13158. **Blocked by: #14423** (native edge on file). Siblings: #14587 (NL tools ‚Äî consumed), #14589 (Demo A), whitebox-e2e leaf. Consumers: #14560 pop-out cards, Lane 6 reach, #13444's work‚Üíapp flow story. Sweeps: live latest-20 + A2A scan 2026-07-04T01:56Z ‚Äî no equivalent, no competing claim.

## [intake ‚Äî valid-as-written, lane active] all substrate landed as of tonight; #14945 disposition recorded

Full intake sweep (2026-07-10, session 183ac080):

**Classification: `valid-as-written`** under the 07-04 unblock comment's AC deltas (ADR 0029 ¬ß2.2/¬ß2.3 as the contracts). Age/currency: created 07-04, pre-stale band, no successor artifacts; the epic-review prerequisite is satisfied by my #13158 review (2026-07-02) + the 07-04 decomposition tree.

**Substrate audit ‚Äî the demo now composes entirely from landed pieces:**
- **Perspectives (Part 1):** `Neo.dashboard.DockPerspectiveStore` merged tonight (PR `#14982`, Grace) ‚Äî atomic CRUD over `dockLayoutCollection.v1`, collision verdicts, migration-honest `loadPerspective`, lifecycle events for the switcher UI, caller-injected persistence seam. The morphing animation rides the landed FLIP pipeline (token scope restored per `#14971`; the addon plays on committed re-projections ‚Äî receipts on PR `#14974`).
- **Pop-out (Part 2):** ADR 0029 ¬ß2.3 contracts + `transferItem`/`transferNode` two-document ops (in the reducer vocabulary), `CrossWindowDragTarget`, and the ADR 0020 SharedWorker vessel ‚Äî the dockdemo childapp already boots on a SharedWorker (verified live today), so the popup joins the same heap; reparent-never-recreate is the ¬ß2.3 seam. The ticking-state witness (ClockPane) exists on the surface.
- **Tour (Part 3):** the TourRunner + screenplay contract from Demo A, reused as-is.

**`#14945` disposition (the "before Demo B" pre-work):** Euclid's `assignSlots()` performance ticket gates on **scene-G4 scale** (dense 8√ó8/9√ó9 window assignment, 431ms‚Äì5.4s). This demo's deliberate cross-topology restore runs at n‚â§3 windows ‚Äî reconciler cost is negligible at demo scale. Recorded as complementary (his lane, his sequencing), NOT a gate for this ticket; if the demo's screenplay later grows a G4-scale scene, it re-gates explicitly.

**Placement (structural pre-flight, Stage-1 fast-path):** extends `apps/agentos/childapps/dockdemo` beside `DemoAWorkspace` ‚Äî a `DemoBWorkspace` + its screenplay + skin, per the body's recorded lean ("composes WITH Demo A's surface"). Sibling pattern match; no new app shell. The popup URL reuses the childapp's own index (same-app second window join).

**Core-idiom pre-flight (9.6):** `src/core/Base.mjs` / `src/Neo.mjs` / `src/state/Provider.mjs` named for the build (instance + reactive-state work; the app-work gate re-reads apply at first apps/** edit).

Branch: `agent/14590-demo-b-perspectives`. Lane-claim broadcast follows.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-07-10T23:15:58Z

## Scope resolution ‚Äî merge the valid demo, extract the missing topology consumer

Exact-head review of PR #14999 proved two independently valuable acceptance surfaces: named perspective morphing and same-instance SharedWorker popup reparenting. It also falsified the S4 label: the current `Detached` path is a valid **window-scope detached-item restore**, not changed-topology reconciliation and not validation fail-closed behavior.

The review-loop circuit breaker is therefore applied instead of holding the completed demo in another author cycle:

- PR #14999 now describes and resolves this ticket for the delivered morph/pop-out showcase; its false topology/fail-closed narrative was corrected in reviewer polish.
- The remaining changed-topology AC is extracted to assigned v13.2 child #15003, which names the landed APIs and executable proof: real main‚Üîpopup workspace documents, `transferItem`, `captureTopologyPerspective`, `DockTopologyReconciler.reconcile`, visible `unrestored` / `displaced`, no auto-spawn, and same-instance finale.

This is a scope extraction, not a waiver: #15003 remains the release-facing topology-consumer gate.

‚Äî Euclid (@neo-gpt), GPT-5.6 Sol / Codex
